channel.title: InsurTech Intelligence
channel.description: Noticias globales de insurtech, actualizadas cada 6 horas.

item.title: El Consejo General de Mediadores reclama equilibrio entre carga regulatoria e interlocución con aseguradoras
item.description: El presidente del Consejo General de Mediadores de Seguros, Javier Barberá, reclamó ayer un mayor diálogo entre corredores y compañías aseguradoras y alertó de que el incremento de obligaciones regulatorias no va acompañado de las condiciones necesarias para ejercer la profesión en igualdad de condiciones . El presidente del Consejo General situó estas tensiones en un contexto de concentración creciente en el mercado asegurador. En esa línea, rechazó cualquier modelo basado en la confrontación entre Mediación y aseguradoras.

item.title: Coseba reafirma su objetivo de triplicar el negocio en 2030
item.description: José Clavellinas, presidente ejecutivo y socio fundador, expuso e l plan estratégico denominado hasta 2030, cuyo objetivo central es triplicar el volumen de negocio registrado a cierre de 2023 . Coseba 1986 celebró del 6 al 10 de mayo su XVII Convención Premier en Agadir (Marruecos). El encuentro reunió a los miembros de su Red Premier y coincidió con el 20.º aniversario de esa red y el 40.º aniversario de la correduría cordobesa.

item.title: La población española suspende en cultura aseguradora
item.description: Un estudio elaborado por YouGov para Mapfr e revela que la comprensión de los términos habituales en los contratos de seguro es limitada entre la población española. La población cuenta con una media de 2,6 seguros. Es decir, existe un segmento de población que mantiene coberturas activas sin verificar periódicamente sus condiciones, renovaciones o exclusiones.

item.title: NCCI: Workers’ Comp Calendar Year Combined Ratio at 91; Accident Year CR 102
item.description: NCCI reported that while the calendar year 2025 combined ratio for workers’ compensation is 91 (compared with 86 the prior year), the workers’ comp accident year 2025 combined ratio is 102 with prior years continuing to experience downward reserve development. She said the rise in 2025 accident year combined ratio is not surprising, adding that California, which accounts for 20% of the workers’ comp industry, reported a 2025 accident year combined ratio of 129. NCCI said that it’s the 12th consecutive year where the workers’ comp calendar year measured below 100, an indication of the line’s overall profitability.

item.title: Adapting Claim Investigations for AI-Driven Fraud
item.description: Admiral, one of the United Kingdom’s largest motor and home insurers, reported a 71% year-over-year increase in fraud in 2025, driven in part by AI-generated evidence. Deepfakes were the phenomenon of 2024, but the synthetic claim has now quickly become the defining fraud trend. Professionals must completely change how they approach claim investigations and decisions.

item.title: Report: Hybrid Vehicle Collision Claims Hit Record High in First Quarter
item.description: The report also shows that in the first quarter, the share of repairable collision claims for battery electric vehicles (BEVs) held steady at 3.33% in the U.S. and 5.28% in Canada for the first quarter, according to Mitchell’s its Q1 2026 Plugged-In: EV Collision Insights report. Claims for mild hybrid electric vehicles surged to record highs in North America, with year-over-year increases of 25% in the U.S.

item.title: Delta Insurance New Zealand hires Petr Dorofeev as tech, cyber product lead
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: ‘Pivotal moment’: Lloyd’s chief says no need for subtlety in soft cycle fight
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Marsh to acquire Japanese energy giant Eneos’ insurance units
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: i.AM Innovation Lab lays groundwork for wider ILS investor base with reusable tokenisation platform
item.description: After helping Schroders Capital and global reinsurer Hannover Re launch their pilot tokenisation project in 2024, i.AM Innovation Lab has used the lessons it learned to help both companies’ integration of a tokenised capability into its insurance-linked securities (ILS) investment platform, which in time will help open the door to broader investor access. During a recent interview with Artemis, Pascal Nägeli, Partner and Co-Founder of i.AM Innovation Lab, and Alec Nikolov, Lead Product & Design, explain how the project evolved from a bespoke pilot to a reusable platform, what it took to make tokenised ILS work at institutional scale, and why they see tokenisation primarily as a cost and efficiency play. Established in 2018 out of a large Swiss asset manager, i.AM Innovation Lab has spent the past few years sitting at the intersection of asset management, technology and insurance-linked securities (ILS).

item.title: Mt. Logan Capital Management hires Yulia Bruskova as Chief Analytics Officer
item.description: (MLCM), the Everest Group owned and operated third-party capital management platform, has hired experienced actuary and analytical expert Yulia Bruskova as its new Chief Analytics Officer. this month, Bruskova takes on the Chief Analytics Officer role, further strengthening the firm’s analytics expertise. During her career, Bruskova has held roles at industry-leading firms, including as Group Actuary at reinsurance firm RenaissanceRe, Head of the Actuarial, Risk and Analytics Practice at Deloitte Bermuda, Chief Risk & Investment Officer at long-tail focused reinsurer F&G Re Ltd., and Group Chief Risk Officer at Aquiline-backed Armour Group.

item.title: S&P assigns A+ rating to MSIG USA’s new E&S platform
item.description: “This rating is an important step in the continued build-out of our specialty platform in the U.S.,” said Peter McKenna, chief executive officer of MSIG USA. MSIG Specialty Insurance America, Inc., MSIG USA’s newly established excess and surplus (E&S) insurer, has received an A+ financial strength rating and issuer credit rating with a stable outlook from S&P Global Ratings. This rating is a significant milestone for MSIG USA and its ongoing development as a “leading data-driven specialty insurer,”, and strengthens its capacity to meet complex and changing risk requirements of its clients.

item.title: Pacific Life Re hires Febby Mulewa as Head of Underwriting and Claims, Protection, Europe
item.description: Life reinsurer Pacific Life Re has announced the appointment of Febby Mulewa as Head of Underwriting and Claims, Protection, Europe, where she will oversee initiatives aimed at driving sustainable growth and operational excellence across the Protection line of business. Mulewa will collaborate closely with the Pacific Life Re global underwriting and claims teams to align and enhance regional performance. During her career, Mulewa has led underwriting and claims strategies to optimise portfolio performance and strengthen risk management.

item.title: RenRe’s Qutub and Curtis to retire at year-end, Neuber named CFO
item.description: RenRe confirms that Matthew Neuber, RenRe’s Senior Financial Officer and Corporate Treasurer, will succeed Qutub as CFO, effective January 1st, 2027. To ensure a smooth transition, Qutub will serve as a strategic advisor to RenRe for 12 months after his retirement, while Curtis will serve as a strategic advisor for six months. Robert “Bob” Qutub, Chief Financial Officer (CFO), and Ross Curtis, Chief Portfolio Officer of Bermuda-domiciled, global reinsurance company, RenaissanceRe Holdings Ltd.

item.title: Illinois Man Receives $300K Settlement From Dog Attack
item.description: An Illinois man who suffered severe injuries to his hand and fingers in a dog attack received a $300,000 settlement, the law firm John J. The man attacked by two pit bulls while taking his German Shepard for a walk in the town of Elgin. The incident occurred when he tried to separate the pit bulls from his dog, at which point he was attacked.

item.title: City of Austin to Pay $35M to Men Wrongly Accused in Yogurt Shop Murders
item.description: The city of Austin will pay $35 million to three men and the family of a fourth who were wrongly accused of the 1991 rape and murder of four teenage girls at a yogurt shop , a case that initially sent one of the men to death row and another to life in prison, under a tentative settlement reached Tuesday. “This settlement closes the final chapter of a devastating story in Austin’s history,” Austin City Manager T.C. “We are pleased to have reached an agreement with those who were wrongly accused and wrongly convicted in this case and hope that this settlement brings a sense of closure to everyone affected by this horrific event.” Scott and his attorney Tony Diaz said in a joint statement they are hopeful the settlement will help improve investigation practices and safeguards against wrongful convictions.

item.title: ‘Extremely unpredictable’: Marsh, Aon warn of critical risk, tighter terms for Strait of Hormuz transits
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Santam taps Hemant Gulati to lead Gift City buildout
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: New Law Requires Insurance Rate Filings in Oklahoma to Undergo Review Process
item.description: Oklahoma Governor Kevin Stitt this week signed a bill that gives the insurance department the authority to review insurance rate changes before they can go into effect. The new law allows the insurance commissioner to review filings and request actuarial data when rates appear excessively high, unfair or discriminatory. Stacy Jo Adams, R-Duncan, said the legislation will provide a strong process to oversee rate increases in a state that faces some of the highest property insurance rates in the country.

item.title: Chicago Museum’s ‘Knight Rider’ Car Ticketed for Speeding in NY Hasn’t Moved in Years
item.description: The Volo Museum near Chicago, which has a replica of the show’s Trans Am that hasn’t moved from its exhibit in years, recently received a $50 traffic ticket from the Big Apple, alleging its car was doing 36 mph (59 kph) in a 25 mph (40 kph) zone in Brooklyn on April 22. What traffic law violator with unpaid fines is driving a black Pontiac Trans Am that looks like the car with the talking computer from the 1980s TV series “Knight Rider,” and even has the same license plate? The ticket came complete with traffic camera photos showing a black Trans Am with the California license plate KNIGHT, the same plate as the car on the show and the novelty one on the museum’s unregistered car.

item.title: LexisNexis launches AI property risk model for home insurers
item.description: LexisNexis Risk Solutions launched Location Intelligence for Home, an AI-driven predictive model designed to help US home insurers improve property risk assessment and underwriting decisions. The model uses neural network technology, industry-wide claims data, location-based insights, and historical loss patterns to generate property-level risk scores across six perils: hail, wind, weather-related water, non-weather-related water, freeze, and collapse or falling object. LexisNexis plans to file the model in multiple states for underwriting and rating use in the coming months.

item.title: Tiger Woods’ Prescription Records Subpoenaed by Prosecutors in Florida DUI Case
item.description: Tiger Woods ‘ prescription drug records will be handed over to prosecutors following his March arrest in Florida on suspicion of driving under the influence, a judge ruled Tuesday morning. Prosecutors had issued a subpoena seeking copies of all prescription medication records for the legendary golfer at a Palm Beach pharmacy from the start of the year through the end of March. Defense attorney Doug Duncan had previously argued that Woods has a constitutional right to privacy when it comes to his prescription medications, but he acknowledged during the hearing that the right is not absolute and that prosecutors could make a compelling argument for why they were needed.

item.title: Forecaster Says El Nino Has 82% Chance of Developing by July
item.description: El Niño is likely to develop soon, with an 82% probability during May-July 2026, the U.S. The weather pattern is expected to continue through the Northern Hemisphere winter, with a 96% chance in December 2026-February 2027, the U.S. “While confidence in the occurrence of El Niño has increased since last month, there is still substantial uncertainty in the peak strength of El Niño, with no strength categorization exceeding a 37% chance,” the U.S.

item.title: California Sees Steady Rise in Work Comp Claim Costs Since 2021: WCRI
item.description: Newly released data highlights how total payments per claim in California’s workers’ compensation system have risen steadily since 2021, according to the Workers Compensation Research Institute (WCRI). California had higher total costs per claim for all paid claims than in other study states due to a higher percentage of claims with more than seven days of lost time. The report, CompScope™ Benchmarks for California, 2026 Edition, authored by William Monnin‑Browder, examines how costs and system performance in California’s workers’ compensation system have changed between 2020 and 2025 in comparison to other states.

item.title: Jio, Allianz incorporate 50:50 general insurance JV in India: report
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Exclusive: Xpanner Lands $18M To Offer ‘Automation As A Service’ To Construction Sites
item.description: Customers subscribe to task-specific automation licenses such as piling, material handling, trenching and grading through XPanner’s Automation-as-a-Service (AaaS) model. Xpanner , a startup automating construction work through robotics and physical AI technology, has raised $18 million in a Series B round, the company tells Crunchbase News exclusively. Xpanner turns construction equipment that customers already own into automated assets “without replacing a single machine,” according to Henri Lee , the company’s co-founder and CEO.

item.title: The Crunchbase Tech Layoffs Tracker
item.description: Methodology This tracker includes layoffs conducted by U.S.-based companies or those with a strong U.S. We source the layoffs from media reports, our own reporting, social media posts and layoffs.fyi , a crowdsourced database of tech layoffs. We recently updated our layoffs tracker to reflect the most recent round of layoffs each company has conducted.

item.title: ADB cat bonds an ‘important proof point’ of Munich Re’s Public Sector objectives: Roth
item.description: After assisting the Asian Development Bank (ADB) in the issuance of its first catastrophe bond offerings, Michael Roth of Munich Re has highlighted how the transactions are an important proof of the reinsurer’s Capital Partners Public Sector objectives. “The transaction is for us another important proof point for the objectives of Munich Re’s CAP Public Sector Business Practice: To combine solutions that permit to contribute to the achievement of SDGs with the development of business for Munich Re. He continued: “ADB brands its new instrument as Disaster Resilient Bonds to highlight the unique characteristics of these bonds: In the case of a triggering event the proceeds of the bonds are directly transferred to the accounts of pre-registered affected, vulnerable households.” Roth also highlights how the transaction serves as a successful “proof of concept” for Munich Re’s Capital Partners public sector practice.

item.title: Hamilton’s fee income generated under Ada Capital Management rises in Q1 2026
item.description: Hamilton Insurance Group, Ltd., a Bermuda based specialty insurance and reinsurance company, has reported higher third-party capital fee income generated by activities under its Ada Capital Management unit in the first-quarter of 2026, while the income may continued to rise this year thanks to the recent casualty sidecar launch from the firm. But, whether this casualty sidecar was active or not, Hamilton’s activities under Ada Capital Management and Ada Re still drove much higher income in Q1 2026. Hamilton has been earning fee income through its Ada Capital Management third-party capital platform and its Ada Re collateralized reinsurance and retrocession underwriting vehicle for a number of years, but with a new sidecar arrangement announced we suspect the run-rate may continue to rise.

item.title: Global property softening met by underwriting discipline and rising ART interest: Aon
item.description: According to broker Aon, underwriting discipline remains intact despite the softening global re/insurance property market, as firms continue to prioritise risk selection and technical pricing, driven by heightened concerns regarding climate-driven natural catastrophes, secondary perils, and evolving geopolitical risks. On the back of favourable 1/1 treaty reinsurance renewals, Aon observed that property insurers entered 2026 with strong balance sheets and ambitious growth targets, accelerating the ongoing softening of the global commercial property insurance market. Insurers are increasingly looking to write larger line sizes while also showing greater willingness to deploy capacity for complex and higher-hazard risks when supported by robust underwriting information.” Whilst there is ample capacity in the market overall, Aon noted that the availability of capacity is still more limited for U.S.-exposed casualty risks, certain commercial automobile risks, and property and casualty risks in Japan.

item.title: Howden Asia appoints Alaric Lee and Alfred Sham to key leadership roles
item.description: Global insurance broker Howden has announced a significant leadership transition for Howden Asia, appointing Alaric Lee as Chief Executive Officer, Hong Kong, and Alfred Sham as Chief Strategy Officer, Asia. Sham brings more than 20 years of industry experience spanning M&A, strategy, and leadership across Asia. Howden said the leadership transition reflects its commitment to developing internal talent and aligning leadership capabilities with its strategic priorities across Asia.

item.title: Accelerant CEO hails AI as ‘meaningful execution enabler’ amid productivity gains
item.description: They’re focused on the core operations of the accelerant risk exchange, and AI has become a meaningful execution enabler for us.” According to Jeff Radke, the company’s use of AI has increased output per engineer and delivered a productivity improvement of more than 24%, which he said is enabling Accelerant to move more quickly, invest in new areas, and advance its strategic priorities more efficiently. Speaking during Accelerant’s Q1 2026 earnings call, Co-Founder and Chief Executive Officer Jeff Radke highlighted the productivity gains the firm is achieving through its use of artificial intelligence, including the development of in-house solutions designed to reduce, and potentially eliminate, its reliance on costly third-party software systems. Having previously emphasised that AI forms a core part of the firm’s architecture and delivers benefits for both members and risk capital partners, Radke said one of the most significant advantages has been the internal productivity gains Accelerant is now generating.

item.title: ICIEC, OeKB strengthen export credit and investment risk coverage with reinsurance agreement
item.description: ICIEC, a Shariah-based multilateral insurer and member of the Islamic Development Bank Group, and OeKB, acting as agent of the Republic of Austria under the Export Guarantees Act, have signed a Framework Reinsurance Agreement to strengthen export credit and investment risk coverage. Under the agreement, the Islamic Corporation for the Insurance of Investment and Export Credit (ICIEC) will provide facultative reinsurance on a case-by-case basis for insurance facilities supported by Oesterreichische Kontrollbank Aktiengesellschaft (OeKB). The agreement blends OeKB’s export credit insurance expertise with ICIEC’s multilateral mandate and specialised regional risk insights.

item.title: Billionaire Boehly’s Allies Donated Heavily to Kansas Insurance Regulator
item.description: Todd Boehly and dozens of his associates contributed more than $300,000 to the Kansas insurance commissioner’s gubernatorial campaign, weeks before her office helped one of the billionaire’s companies win a delay of new capital rules. A slew of Boehly’s companies, including Topeka-based Security Benefit Life Insurance Co., donated en masse in late December, joined by executives and their spouses and other relatives, Kansas records show . Commissioner Vicki Schmidt is in charge of regulating Kansas insurance companies and is seeking the Republican nomination for governor.

item.title: Newsom’s California Budget Bolstered by Extra Cash from AI Boom
item.description: Gavin Newsom unveiled a revised budget that shows no deficit for this year and next, as the state draws another boost from the technology and artificial-intelligence boom. But the surge in revenue doesn’t erase California’s longer-term financial problems, leaving the next governor to manage a budget that remains vulnerable to any reversal in markets. Related: States Across Wildfire-Prone West Are Using AI for Early Detection The May revision builds on Newsom’s January proposal, which called for a $349 billion spending plan for the fiscal year beginning in July and narrowed the projected deficit to about $3 billion as stronger cash receipts, resilient financial markets and a better economic outlook lifted revenue.

item.title: El Niño Odds Rise as Storm and Crop Threats Intensify Globally
item.description: The odds are rising that a weather-roiling El Niño will emerge in the next few months and strengthen through the year, threatening global crop supplies, altering storm patterns and pushing temperatures toward record highs. A powerful El Niño in 1997, which some observers think this year’s event could rival, killed at least 30,000 people and caused an estimated $100 billion in damage globally. There is an 82% chance El Niño will develop in the equatorial Pacific between May and July, and a 67% chance it will be either a strong or very strong event when it peaks between November to January, the US Climate Prediction Center said in a Thursday statement.

item.title: MACM selects OneShield for core system modernisation
item.description: Medical Assurance Company of Mississippi (MACM) has chosen OneShield Market Solutions as its new end-to-end core insurance platform as it shifts from a legacy on-premises setup to a cloud-based system. “Moving to OneShield is not just a technology upgrade it’s a strategic investment in MACM’s future. Founded in 1976 during a period of medical malpractice insurance market stress in Mississippi, MACM provides coverage for around 2,500 physicians across the state, alongside risk management services and claims support.

item.title: MS&AD to acquire 18% stake in Barings from MassMutual for $1.44 billion
item.description: Deal strengthens ties between the insurers and supports long-term growth strategy MassMutual has agreed to sell an 18% equity stake in its global asset management subsidiary Barings to MS&AD Insurance Group, in a transaction valued at approximately $1.44 billion. Following completion, MassMutual will retain an 82% controlling stake in Barings and continue to hold governance control. Despite the minority sale, Barings will continue to manage the bulk of MassMutual’s general investment account and remain central to its overall asset management framework.

item.title: Hub expands life sciences practice in North America
item.description: HUB International expanded its Life Sciences Practice in the US and Canada, adding integrated risk advisory services spanning business insurance, risk services, employee benefits, and retirement consulting. The practice is designed to support life sciences organizations at different stages of growth, from startups running initial clinical trials to multinational companies managing product liability and regulatory exposures across jurisdictions. The expansion comes as life sciences companies face increasingly complex risks, including funding volatility, governance gaps, regulatory scrutiny, and reputational exposures that extend beyond traditional insurance coverage.

item.title: Protective announces CEO Rich Bielen’s plans to retire
item.description: Protective Life, a subsidiary of Daiichi Life Group, announced that Rich Bielen, Chief Executive Officer, has shared his plans to retire at the end of 2026. Protective also announced that Scott Adams, Executive Vice President, Chief Transformation & Strategy Officer, and Mark Drew, Executive Vice President, Chief Legal Officer, long‑tenured members of the company’s executive leadership team, have shared their plans to retire at the end of 2026. Paul has been an integral part of Protective’s growth strategy for many years, and as I look ahead, I have great confidence in him, our leadership team and the strong foundation we’ve built together to support the company’s continued success.” – Rich Bielen.

item.title: Neptune prices secondary offering at $27.50 per share
item.description: Neptune Flood parent Neptune Insurance Holdings announced the pricing of a secondary public offering of 9.84 million shares of Class A common stock at $27.50 per share. As part of the offering, Neptune intends to buy back 984,140 shares from the underwriters at $26.40 per share, with the repurchased shares set to be retired. The shares are being sold by existing securityholders, meaning Neptune itself will not receive proceeds from the sale, aside from a concurrent share repurchase transaction.

item.title: OpenAI Floats Idea of Global AI Governance Body With US, China
item.description: OpenAI would support the creation of a global governance body for artificial intelligence led by the U.S. While Lehane said that OpenAI has floated in Washington the idea of linking AI safety institutes, it’s unclear whether the Trump administration would welcome global guidelines that include China. When asked about the China summit, OpenAI’s Vice President of Global Affairs Chris Lehane said Wednesday that the U.S.

item.title: Can Trump’s New FEMA Pick Save the Beleaguered Disaster Agency?
item.description: President Donald Trump’s pick to lead the Federal Emergency Management Agency wrote a memo last spring laying out how to kill the disaster agency. When called before Congress, he endorsed keeping the agency, not ending it as outlined in his own memo, titled “ Abolishing FEMA .” That publicly put him at odds with his two bosses, Trump and Kristi Noem, then Homeland Security secretary. If confirmed, Hamilton will face a summer of climate-fueled threats from floods, fires and heat waves while “inheriting an agency that is grossly understaffed, where the morale is probably underwater,” said Pete Gaynor, a veteran emergency manager from Rhode Island who served as Trump’s first-term FEMA chief.

item.title: MS&AD completes acquisition of 18% equity stake in Barings
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Soft market deepens dilemma at BIBA as brokers navigate rising risk, cyber demands
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Working together against financial crime
item.description: Which is why the fight against financial crime sits at the heart of our 5-year strategy . Speech by Nikhil Rathi, FCA chief executive at the FCA's financial crime conference. Speaker: Nikhil Rathi, chief executive Event: FCA financial crime conference Delivered: 14 May 2026 Note: This is a drafted speech and may differ from the delivered version It’s more technologically enabled.

item.title: Supporting customers through challenging times
item.description: We want to remind you about our clear expectations on the support you should offer consumers in challenging times, through the Consumer Duty and our rules on protections for borrowers in difficulty . The Duty requires firms to put customers’ needs first and avoid foreseeable harm, including those in vulnerable circumstances. This includes checking that product features are not causing harm, including to customers in or approaching financial difficulty, or to those who need extra help.

item.title: April’s Most Active US Investors Included Some Usual Suspects, And Some Unusual Ones
item.description: For a bigger-picture view, below we list the top X most active lead investors for April: That’s reflected in April’s tally of most active U.S. We’ll start with most active venture investors, looking at those who participated in the largest number of startup funding rounds for $5 million or more at any stage.

item.title: Nationwide Mutual lifts Aquila Re I 2026-1 cat bond target again, to as much as $350m
item.description: We have now been told by sources, that Nationwide has lifted the target size a little further for this Aquila Re I Series 2026-1 cat bond sponsorship to seek between $325 million and as much as $350 million of reinsurance. Initially, Nationwide Mutual was aiming to secure $200 million of multi-peril and multi-year indemnity triggered and per-occurrence based reinsurance protection from this Aquila Re I 2026-1 cat bond transaction. The two tranches of Aquila Re I Series 2026-1 cat bond notes on offer will protect Nationwide Mutual against losses from the perils of US named storm, earthquake, severe thunderstorm, winter storm, wildfire, meteorite impact, volcanic eruption.

item.title: Icosa to add Dell’Amore as a Portfolio Manager for growing cat bond fund
item.description: Icosa Investments AG is adding experienced insurance-linked securities and catastrophe bond investments specialist Raffaele Dell’Amore as a new Portfolio Manager for its growing Icosa Cat Bond Fund strategy, Artemis has learned. We understand Dell’Amore will be employed by both Icosa Investments AG, which distributes the Icosa Cat Bond Fund UCITS strategy, as well as its fund management partner MRB Fund Partners AG which undertakes the regulated portfolio management activities, and is set to join as a PM for the Icosa Cat Bond Fund, working alongside its CEO and founder Florian Steiger in July 2026. That company was later acquired by asset management group Schroders and from 2015 to 2019 Dell’Amore worked as a portfolio manager for its private ILS strategies.
